project.name: singbox-ui
project.description: A full-stack sing-box configuration management application with a Go/Gin backend and Next.js/React frontend. The backend handles Docker container orchestration, subscription parsing (Clash/SS/Trojan/VLESS/VMess), proxy probing and speed testing, WireGuard/WARP configuration, certificate generation, and scheduling. The frontend provides visual form-based configuration for all major proxy protocols across inbound and outbound directions, DNS settings, routing rules, and subscription management with a Monaco JSON editor and i18n support for English and Chinese.

nodes.label: .dockerignore
nodes.summary: Docker build context exclusion rules. Excludes node_modules, .next, Go build artifacts, git history, IDE configs, docs, OS files, logs, and temp directories to keep the Docker build context lean.
nodes.name: service:.dockerignore

nodes.label: docker-compose.yml
nodes.summary: Docker Compose orchestration for singbox-ui service. Uses ghcr.io/spadesa99/singbox_ui:latest image with host networking, Docker socket bind mount, data volume, and environment configuration for DATA_DIR, HOST_DATA_DIR, LISTEN_ADDR, and TZ.
nodes.name: service:docker-compose.yml

nodes.label: Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Multi-stage Docker build: frontend stage (Node 22, Next.js build), builder stage (Go cross-compilation with CGO disabled), runtime stage (Alpine with ca-certificates, tzdata, singbox-image.tar). Exposes port 8080, runs sing-box-ui binary.
nodes.name: service:Dockerfile

nodes.label: docker-build.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline that builds and pushes a multi-architecture (linux/amd64, linux/arm64) Docker image to ghcr.io. Triggers on push to master/main/feature/sing-box, version tags, and PRs. Builds with Buildx, pulls sing-box base images from ghcr.io/sagernet/sing-box, and caches via GitHub Actions cache.
nodes.name: docker-build.yml

nodes.summary: Agent development rules for the sing-box UI project. Mandates running linters (golangci-lint for Go, ESLint for frontend) and compilation checks before any commit. Requires creating a dedicated git branch (feat/<short-description>) before writing design specifications under docs/superpowers/specs/. Enforces no commits on main/master.
nodes.name: AGENTS.md

nodes.summary: Comprehensive development guide for Claude Code AI assistant working with the sing-box UI repository. Covers approach philosophy, project overview (Go backend with embedded Next.js frontend, Docker-in-Docker pattern), development commands (frontend with Next.js 16, backend with Go 1.24+ Gin), architecture details (build flow embedding frontend in Go binary, key directories, Docker-in-Docker container management, prober service design with goroutines and ring buffer), API structure (/api/ prefix endpoints for singbox, wireguard, subscription, prober), environment variables (DATA_DIR, HOST_DATA_DIR, LISTEN_ADDR, TZ), and sing-box configuration format details including differences from Xray-core (action field, rule_set, DNS type field, outbound tag convention proxy_out, route rule actions).
nodes.name: CLAUDE.md

nodes.summary: Chinese-language project README for sing-box UI, a modern web-based sing-box configuration management tool. Covers core features: protocol support (inbound/outbound/subscription), TLS certificate management (ACME auto-cert + manual), route rule configuration (quick templates, direct mode), load balancing (URLTest mode with configurable tolerance), multi-instance management, WireGuard VPN management (Curve25519 key gen, client config), Cloudflare WARP outbound (one-click registration, endpoint optimization via real WG handshake probing, wide coverage scanning), node health probing (async, sliding window). Includes quick start with Docker Compose, remote access via SSH tunnel, environment variables reference, tech stack table (Next.js 16, React 19, Go 1.24, Gin 1.11), license (MIT), and acknowledgments.
nodes.name: README.md

nodes.summary: English-language project README for sing-box UI, a modern web-based sing-box configuration management tool. Mirror of README.md in English. Covers features (protocol support, TLS certificate management, route rule configuration, load balancing, multi-instance management, WireGuard VPN, Cloudflare WARP outbound, node health probing), quick start with Docker Compose, remote access via SSH tunnel, environment variables, tech stack, MIT license, and acknowledgments.
nodes.name: README_EN.md

properties.title: API Documentation (Swagger/OpenAPI) Implementation Plan
properties.summary: Plan to add automatic OpenAPI (Swagger) documentation to the Go backend using swaggo annotations, served via Swagger UI at /swagger/index.html. Covers 8 tasks: foundation setup, then annotating each domain (certificate/reality, speedtest, wireguard, warp, subscription, prober, singbox) and system endpoints.

properties.title: Backend Vertical Slices Implementation Plan
properties.summary: Plan to migrate backend from layered architecture (handlers/services) to 8 vertical slice domain packages in internal/, with shared infrastructure in internal/pkg/. 100% public API test coverage required. Covers shared packages (types, config, docker), then independent domains (certificate, wireguard, warp, subscription, prober, speedtest, singbox, scheduler), then main.go rewrite and old package deletion.

properties.title: Native Runtime Implementation Plan
properties.summary: Plan to add NativeRuntime (process-based via os/exec) alongside existing DockerRuntime for sing-box lifecycle, selected via Go build tags (openwrt → native, default → Docker). Introduces Runtime and TempRuntime interfaces with two implementations each. Covers interface definition, DockerRuntime, NativeRuntime, refactoring singbox and speedtest services, main.go updates, and Makefile cross-compile targets.

properties.title: Optional Dashboard Implementation Plan
properties.summary: Plan to add --dashboard CLI flag to make frontend serving optional (default disabled). Covers adding flag to main.go, reverting env var changes from config, updating docker-compose.yml, and final verification.

nodes.name: API Documentation Design (Swagger/OpenAPI)
nodes.summary: Design spec for adding automatic OpenAPI (Swagger) documentation to the Go backend using swaggo. Covers annotation patterns for ~57 endpoints across 8 domains, a standard ErrorResponse type, phased implementation (Phase 0-8), route registration in main.go, and testing/verification steps. Named request/response types (models.go) replace anonymous inline structs.

nodes.name: Backend Vertical Slices Architecture Design
nodes.summary: Architecture migration spec to refactor the Go backend from a layered God-package (services/ + handlers/) to vertical slices. Each domain (singbox, subscription, prober, speedtest, certificate, wireguard, warp, scheduler) becomes an isolated package with handler/service/models/interfaces. Shared infrastructure lives in internal/pkg/{docker,config,types}. Dependency injection in main.go. 11-step migration plan, testing strategy requiring 100% public method coverage across 4 test layers, and interface contracts for cross-domain communication.

nodes.name: Native Runtime: Non-Docker Sing-Box & OpenWrt Support
nodes.summary: Design spec for supporting sing-box as a native OS process alongside existing Docker mode. Introduces a Runtime interface with DockerRuntime (default, !openwrt build tag) and NativeRuntime (openwrt build tag) implementations. Covers process lifecycle, PID file layout, log capture, binary path resolution, and a TempRuntime interface for speedtest. CLI flag --singbox-bin. Cross-compilation for OpenWrt MIPS/ARM targets with conditional Docker SDK exclusion.

nodes.name: Optional Dashboard & External API Access
nodes.summary: Design spec for making the web frontend dashboard optional via a --dashboard CLI flag (default: off). Enables backend-only deployments where a TUI client connects from another machine. External API access via LISTEN_ADDR=0.0.0.0:7000 (CORS already permissive). Docker Compose examples for both backend-only and frontend+backend scenarios.

edges.description: Makefile targets (build, test, tidy) invoke Go toolchain which resolves module from go.mod
go.sum is derived from go.mod and locks checksums for every dependency listed in go.mod
go.mod declares module path singbox-config-service which contains server/main.go as the entry point
Makefile build target compiles the Go package at server/., producing the singbox-ui-linux binary from main.go
go mod tidy generates/updates go.sum based on the dependencies required by go.mod
layout.tsx imports globals.css for global styles
i18n module loads English locale messages
i18n module loads Chinese locale messages
Sibling locale translation files for the same message catalog
Same OpenAPI specification in JSON and YAML formats
Tailwind theme config provides design tokens consumed by globals.css
PostCSS plugin config activates the Tailwind CSS plugin with this theme
Next.js build pipeline consumes PostCSS configuration
Requirements document references Docker multi-stage build and embedded frontend deployment
Swagger spec and requirements document both describe the same API endpoints
DNS config component uses i18n for localized UI labels
Git submodule references the sing-box project managed by this UI

layers.name: Infrastructure
layers.description: Docker containerization, CI/CD pipelines, and build/deployment configuration.

layers.name: Backend Entry
layers.description: Application entry point that initializes the Gin engine, configures middleware, and registers all route groups.

layers.name: Backend API Layer
layers.description: Gin HTTP handlers and route registration that define REST API endpoints for all domains.

layers.name: Backend Service Layer
layers.description: Core business logic: sing-box runtime management, subscription parsing, WARP scanning, WireGuard key management, certificate lifecycle, node probing engine, speedtest execution, and scheduler orchestration.

layers.name: Backend Data Layer
layers.description: Data models, types, and persistence layer including JSON file-based store and domain-specific model definitions.

layers.name: Backend Support
layers.description: Shared infrastructure: Docker client, configuration, shared type definitions, Swagger docs, and Go module configuration.

layers.name: Frontend Entry
layers.description: Next.js app root layout, global styles, providers (i18n), and toast notification system wired at the app level.

layers.name: Frontend UI Layer
layers.description: Main application page composing all configuration panels and system controls.

layers.name: Frontend Components
layers.description: Domain-specific configuration components for inbound/outbound protocols, DNS, routing rules, subscription management, and JSON editor.

layers.name: Frontend State & API
layers.description: Zustand store for sing-box configuration, API client for backend communication, i18n provider, and shared utility functions.

layers.name: Frontend UI Primitives
layers.description: Shared Radix UI-based primitive components (button, card, dialog, input, select, tabs, badge, alert-dialog, checkbox, label, textarea) with Tailwind styling.

layers.name: Frontend Configuration
layers.description: Next.js, PostCSS, and Tailwind CSS build configuration; package manifest and TypeScript config; i18n message files (en/zh).

layers.name: Documentation
layers.description: Project README (en/zh), agent development guidelines (AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md), functional requirements, and design specs for planned features.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start with the README (English and Chinese) to understand the project's purpose: a modern web-based sing-box configuration management tool. Covers protocol support, TLS certificate management, route rules, load balancing, WireGuard VPN, Cloudflare WARP, and node health probing.

tour.title: Infrastructure & Deployment
tour.description: Explore how the application is containerized and deployed. The multi-stage Dockerfile builds the frontend (Node.js) and backend (Go) into a single Alpine image. Docker Compose orchestrates the service with host networking, Docker socket bind mount, and environment variables. The GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline builds multi-architecture images and pushes to ghcr.io.

tour.title: Backend Entry Point & Architecture
tour.description: The Go backend starts at server/main.go. It initializes configuration, creates all domain services (singbox, subscription, warp, wireguard, certificate, prober, speedtest, scheduler), sets up the Gin HTTP engine with CORS middleware, registers API route groups under /api, mounts Swagger documentation at /swagger, and conditionally serves the embedded frontend dist via the --dashboard flag.

tour.title: Backend API Layer
tour.description: Each domain package has a register.go file that defines its REST API routes on the Gin router. Handlers parse HTTP requests, delegate to services, and return JSON responses. Key domains: subscription management (CRUD + URL validation), sing-box instance lifecycle, WireGuard key generation, WARP registration/scanner, speed test execution, certificate (ACME + self-signed), and node health probing.

tour.title: Backend Service Layer
tour.description: The service layer contains core business logic organized in vertical slice packages. The subscription service fetches remote proxy lists and stores parsed nodes. The singbox service manages instance lifecycle (Docker or native runtime) and config persistence. The warp service handles WARP registration, licensing, and endpoint optimization via real WireGuard handshake probing. The wireguard service generates Curve25519 keys and client configs. Interfaces define cross-domain contracts like SubscriptionUpdater, ProbeResultSaver, and SpeedTestResultSaver.

tour.title: Backend Data Layer & Subscription Parsers
tour.description: Data is persisted using JSON file-based stores (FileStore for subscriptions). Each domain defines its own models/structs. The subscription package includes a suite of protocol-specific URI parsers: Clash YAML, Shadowsocks (SIP002), Trojan, VLESS (with Reality support), and VMess. Shared utilities handle base64 decoding and proxy link line parsing. All parsers have corresponding test files.

tour.title: Frontend Entry & Configuration
tour.description: The Next.js 16 frontend starts at app/layout.tsx which sets up the Inter font, global CSS (Tailwind with custom design system), Providers wrapper (i18n context), and toast notifications. Next.js config enables static export mode, React strict mode, and API proxy rewrites. Tailwind config extends the theme with glass/glow effects, custom animations, and a SaaS-style design system.

tour.title: Frontend Main Page & Domain Components
tour.description: The main page (page.tsx) is the heart of the UI, composing all configuration panels: inbound protocol forms (VMess, VLESS, Trojan, Shadowsocks, Hysteria2, TUIC, WireGuard, Naive, HTTP, AnyTLS, Mixed, ShadowTLS), outbound protocol forms, DNS configuration (servers, rules, templates, global settings), route rules (block, direct, proxy, GFW, CN-domain, CN-IP tabs), subscription manager, JSON raw editor, and language switcher. Each major domain uses a barrel index file to compose sub-components.

tour.title: Frontend State Management, API Client & i18n
tour.description: State is managed with a Zustand store (singbox-config.ts) that mirrors the sing-box configuration format with all type definitions for log options, inbounds, outbounds, routing, DNS, TLS, and more. The API client (lib/api.ts) provides typed functions for all backend endpoints. i18n is handled via next-intl with English and Chinese locale message files. UI primitives build on Radix UI with Tailwind styling.

tour.title: Documentation, Architecture & Design Specs
tour.description: The project includes comprehensive documentation: AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md for AI-assisted development workflows, a functional requirements document, and design specifications for planned features including vertical slices architecture migration, native (non-Docker) runtime support for OpenWrt, optional dashboard flag, and OpenAPI/Swagger API documentation. The Swagger spec at /swagger documents ~57 endpoints across 8 API domains.
